# About SkyNest Airlines

SkyNest Airlines was founded by Jeffy Joseph, Aswitha, Sandra and Lakshmi in 2018 and developed exclusively for Royal Caribbean Group as a technology-first airline designed to reimagine global travel from the ground up. Headquartered in Mumbai, India, SkyNest was built on the belief that booking a flight, hotel, and car should take minutes — not hours — and that AI should handle the complexity so travellers can focus on the experience.

The airline launched its first route — Mumbai to Dubai — in March 2019 with two aircraft and a crew of 40. By 2022, SkyNest had expanded to 18 international routes spanning Asia, the Middle East, Europe, and North America, serving over 2.4 million passengers.

In 2023, SkyNest launched its **AI Travel Intelligence Platform** — combining generative AI, retrieval-augmented generation, and agentic trip planning to give every customer a personalised travel advisor at no extra cost.

## As of 2026, SkyNest Operates

About SkyNest Airlines

- **32 international routes** across 8 major global destinations
- **A fleet of 48 aircraft** including Boeing 787 Dreamliners, Airbus A380s, Boeing 777s, and A320neo family
- **Partnerships with 340+ hotels** across Budget, Standard, Premium, and Luxury tiers
- **Car rental agreements** with 12 global partners including SkyRent, LuxuryCars, EcoRide, and BritDrive
- **The SkyNest Miles loyalty programme** with over 1.8 million active members

## Destinations Served

| Destination | Hub Airport | IATA |
|---|---|---|
| India (Mumbai / Delhi / Bangalore / Chennai) | BOM / DEL / BLR / MAA | Multiple |
| Dubai | Dubai International | DXB |

| London | Heathrow | LHR |

## Company Values

About SkyNest Airlines

- **AI-First**: Every touchpoint is supported by intelligent automation and personalisation.
- **Warm Hospitality**: Indian-rooted warmth blended with global efficiency.
- **Sustainability**: Targeting carbon-neutral operations by 2030 through sustainable aviation fuel (SAF) and offset programmes.
- **Inclusivity**: Multi-language support in 14 languages across all customer-facing platforms.

## Office Locations

SkyNest Miles is SkyNest's loyalty programme. Every time you fly, stay, or rent with SkyNest, you earn Miles that can be redeemed for flights, upgrades, hotel nights, and exclusive experiences.

**Active Members**: 1.8 million+  
**Programme Launch**: June 2019  
**Miles Validity**: 3 years from date of earning (reset upon any earning activity)

---

# SkyNest Privacy Policy

**Effective Date**: January 1, 2026  
**Last Updated**: March 1, 2026

---

## 1. Introduction

SkyNest Airlines Pvt Ltd ("SkyNest", "we", "us") respects your privacy and is committed to protecting your personal data. This Privacy Policy explains how we collect, use, store, and share your information when you use our website (www.skynest.aero), mobile app, or any SkyNest service.

---

## 2. Data We Collect

### 2.1 Information You Provide
- Full name, date of birth, nationality, passport/ID number (required for bookings)
- Contact information: email address, phone number, mailing address
- Payment information (processed via PCI-DSS compliant third-party gateways — we do not store full card numbers)
- Travel preferences, dietary requirements, accessibility needs
- Feedback, reviews, and survey responses

### 2.2 Information Collected Automatically
- Device type, operating system, browser, IP address
- App usage patterns, pages visited, session duration
- Location data (when app location permission is granted)
- Cookies and tracking pixels (see Cookie Policy below)

SkyNest Privacy Policy

### 2.3 Information from Third Parties
- Partner hotel and car rental booking confirmations
- Visa service provider verifications
- Government watch-list screening data (mandatory for all airlines)

---

## 3. How We Use Your Data

| Purpose | Legal Basis |
|---|---|
| Booking management and flight operations | Contractual necessity |
| Identity verification and fraud prevention | Legal obligation |
| Personalised AI travel recommendations | Legitimate interest |
| Marketing communications (opt-in only) | Consent |
| Analytics to improve services | Legitimate interest |
| Loyalty programme management | Contractual necessity |
| Government and security reporting | Legal obligation |

---

## 4. Data Sharing

SkyNest Privacy Policy

We share your data with:
- **Airlines and airports**: Mandatory for operations, check-in, immigration
- **Partner hotels and car rental companies**: Only booking-relevant data
- **Payment processors**: Stripe, Razorpay, PayU (PCI-DSS compliant)
- **Government authorities**: When legally required (immigration, customs, security screening)
- **AI platform providers**: Anthropic Claude (anonymised/aggregated data only for AI Copilot)

We **never sell** your personal data to advertisers or data brokers.

---

## 5. Data Retention

- **Booking records**: 7 years (legal and tax requirements)
- **Passport / ID scans**: Deleted 90 days after travel completion
- **Marketing preferences**: Until you unsubscribe
- **App usage logs**: 24 months rolling window

---

## 6. Your Rights

You have the right to:
- **Access**: Request a copy of all data we hold about you
- **Correction**: Fix inaccurate data
- **Erasure**: Request deletion (subject to legal retention requirements)
- **Portability**: Receive your data in machine-readable format
- **Opt-out**: Withdraw consent for marketing at any time

SkyNest Privacy Policy

To exercise rights: privacy@skynest.aero

---

## 7. Cookies

We use:
- **Essential cookies**: Required for booking functionality
- **Analytics cookies**: Google Analytics, Mixpanel (you may opt out)
- **Personalisation cookies**: Power AI recommendations (you may opt out)

Manage cookie preferences at: www.skynest.aero/cookie-settings

---

## 8. Contact

**Data Protection Officer**: dpo@skynest.aero  
**Registered Office**: SkyNest Airlines Pvt Ltd, Level 12, One BKC, Bandra Kurla Complex, Mumbai 400051, India

# SkyNest Refund Policy

**Effective Date**: January 1, 2026  
**Policy Version**: 4.2

---

## 1. Overview

SkyNest Airlines is committed to fair, transparent refund processing. This policy applies to all tickets purchased directly through SkyNest — via the website, app, or customer care. Tickets purchased through third-party agencies are subject to that agency's refund terms.

---

## 2. Refund Eligibility by Fare Type

### Economy — Flexi Fare
- **Cancellation before departure**: Full refund minus a $45 processing fee
- **Cancellation within 24 hours of booking**: 100% refund, no fee (provided departure is 7+ days away)
- **No-show**: No refund. Travel credit of 20% fare value issued for future SkyNest bookings (valid 12 months)

### Economy — Saver Fare
- **Cancellation 72+ hours before departure**: 50% refund
- **Cancellation within 72 hours of departure**: No refund. Travel credit of 15% fare value
- **No-show**: No refund. No travel credit issued.

SkyNest Refund Policy

### Business Class
- **Cancellation before departure**: Full refund minus $75 processing fee
- **Cancellation within 24 hours of booking**: 100% refund, no fee
- **No-show**: 30% of base fare refunded as SkyNest Miles

### First Class (select routes)
- **Cancellation before departure**: Full refund minus $100 processing fee
- **No-show**: 40% of base fare refunded as SkyNest Miles

---

## 3. Refund Processing Timeline

| Payment Method | Refund Timeline |
|---|---|
| Credit / Debit Card | 7–10 business days |
| Net Banking / UPI | 5–7 business days |
| SkyNest Wallet | Instant (within 2 hours) |
| SkyNest Miles redemption | Instant re-credit |
| Cash / Bank Transfer | 10–15 business days |

---

## 4. Non-Refundable Items

The following are **never** refunded regardless of circumstances:
- Airport taxes and government levies (these are remitted to authorities)
- Fuel surcharges on Saver fares
- Add-on services (seat upgrades purchased separately, in-flight Wi-Fi passes, lounge access purchased independently)
- Visa assistance fee ($15 service charge)

---

SkyNest Refund Policy

## 5. Involuntary Changes (Airline-Initiated)

If SkyNest cancels, delays by more than 3 hours, or materially reschedules your flight:

- **Full refund** of all amounts paid (including taxes, surcharges, and add-ons)
- **OR** free rebooking on next available SkyNest flight to same destination, same cabin
- **Compensation**: $50 voucher for delays of 3–6 hours; $150 voucher for delays of 6+ hours; $300 voucher for cancellations

---

## 6. How to Request a Refund

1. Log in to **My Bookings** on www.skynest.aero or the app
2. Select the booking → click **Cancel & Refund**
3. Choose refund destination (original payment method or SkyNest Wallet)
4. Confirmation email sent within 1 hour

Alternatively, contact Customer Care: care@skynest.aero or 1800-SKY-NEST.

---

## 7. SkyNest Miles Members — Additional Benefits

- **Gold members**: Processing fee waived on all Economy refunds
- **Platinum members**: Processing fee waived on all refunds; no-show policy relaxed (50% refund on Flexi fares)
- **Elite members**: Full refunds on all fare types, no exceptions. Dedicated refund priority queue.

# SkyNest Airlines — Terms and Conditions

**Effective Date**: January 1, 2026

---

## 1. Agreement to Terms

By booking a flight, hotel, car rental, or package with SkyNest Airlines, you agree to these Terms and Conditions in full. If you do not agree, do not proceed with a booking.

---

## 2. Booking and Ticketing

- All bookings are subject to availability and fare rules applicable at time of purchase.
- A booking is confirmed only upon receipt of full payment and issuance of a booking confirmation email with PNR (Passenger Name Record).
- Passenger names must exactly match government-issued ID. Name corrections (minor typos up to 3 characters) may be made for a $25 fee. Name transfers are not permitted.

## 3. Check-In and Boarding

SkyNest Airlines — Terms and Conditions

- **Online Check-In**: Opens 48 hours before departure, closes 2 hours before.

  - Economy: Check-in closes 60 minutes before departure
  - Business / First: Check-in closes 45 minutes before departure
- Passengers arriving after check-in closure will be denied boarding. No refund is provided for missed flights due to late arrival.

## 4. Baggage

Full baggage policy available in the Baggage Policy document. Key terms:

## 5. Passenger Conduct

SkyNest Airlines — Terms and Conditions

SkyNest reserves the right to refuse carriage or remove any passenger who:
- Is intoxicated, disruptive, or threatening to crew or other passengers
- Refuses to comply with crew safety instructions
- Attempts to smoke (including e-cigarettes) on board
- Tampers with aircraft safety equipment

Offending passengers may be banned from future SkyNest travel and reported to relevant authorities.

## 6. Liability Limitations

- For flight delays and cancellations, SkyNest liability is limited to the compensation amounts specified in our Refund Policy.

## 7. Governing Law

These Terms are governed by the laws of India. Any disputes shall be submitted to the exclusive jurisdiction of courts in Mumbai, India, or resolved via binding arbitration under the Arbitration and Conciliation Act, 1996 (India).

---

## 8. Changes to Terms

SkyNest Airlines — Terms and Conditions

SkyNest may update these Terms at any time. Changes apply only to bookings made after the effective date of the updated Terms.

# Bangkok — SkyNest Destination Guide

## Overview
Bangkok is a city that overwhelms all the senses simultaneously — golden temple spires above a tuk-tuk exhaust haze, the smell of pad thai from a street cart, neon signs reflecting in the Chao Phraya river at night. Thailand's capital is one of the world's top tourist destinations and the gateway to the kingdom's beaches, jungles, and ancient ruins.

## Hub Airport
**Bangkok Suvarnabhumi International (BKK)** — Terminal for SkyNest: International Arrivals, Level 2. Airport Rail Link to central Bangkok: 30 minutes, THB 45. Taxi: metered (~THB 200–350 to most hotels + THB 75 expressway tolls). Grab: ~THB 250–500 to city.

---

## Best Time to Visit
| Season | Months | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Cool & Dry (Best) | Nov – Feb | 25–32°C; ideal for sightseeing; very busy |
| Hot Season | Mar – May | 35–40°C; Songkran water festival (Apr 13–15) |
| Monsoon | Jun – Oct | Daily rain but brief; fewer tourists; best hotel deals |

---

## Must-See Attractions

Bangkok — SkyNest Destination Guide

**Grand Palace & Temples**: Grand Palace + Wat Phra Kaew (Emerald Buddha) — must book online; Wat Pho (giant reclining Buddha, world-famous massage school); Wat Arun (Temple of Dawn — sunrise and sunset)
**Culture & Markets**: Chatuchak Weekend Market (10,000 stalls, every product imaginable), Maeklong Railway Market, Damnoen Saduak Floating Market, Khao San Road (backpacker hub)
**Modern Bangkok**: ICONSIAM riverfront mall (indoor floating market), Asiatique The Riverfront (night market + Ferris wheel), MahaNakhon SkyWalk (78th floor glass tray), Jodd Fairs (trendy night market)
**Food**: Pad Thai at Thipsamai (the original), Tom Yum Goong at P'Aor, Mango sticky rice at Or Tor Kor market, Jay Fai (Michelin-starred street food, THB 1,000+ per dish — reserve 6 months ahead)
**Day Trips**: Ayutthaya UNESCO ruins (1.5 hours north by train/bus), Kanchanaburi (Bridge on the River Kwai, waterfalls), Pattaya beach resort (1.5 hours south)

---

Bangkok — SkyNest Destination Guide

## Getting Around
- **BTS Skytrain**: Elevated rail covering Sukhumvit and Silom corridors; efficient; Rabbit card recommended
- **MRT Subway**: Covers additional areas including Chatuchak, Chinatown
- **Tuk-tuks**: Iconic but negotiate before (or use Grab Tuk-tuk for metered version)
- **Chao Phraya Express Boat**: Scenic and cheap (THB 15–40); connects riverside temples
- **Grab**: Essential app for reliable metered rides; avoids taxi negotiation issues
- **SkyNest Car Rental**: Available near Suvarnabhumi airport; note: traffic in Bangkok is very heavy; Grab strongly recommended for city travel

---

## Currency
Thai Baht (THB). USD 1 ≈ THB 35 (2026). ATMs widely available (THB 220 foreign fee standard). Supermarkets and malls accept cards; street food is cash only.

---

Bangkok — SkyNest Destination Guide

## Practical Tips
- Never disrespect the monarchy — it is illegal in Thailand (lèse-majesté)
- Remove shoes before entering temples; cover shoulders and knees
- The wai (pressed palms greeting) — acknowledge with a smile and slight nod
- Bargaining is expected at markets; start at 50% of asking price and negotiate pleasantly
- Street food is safe and outstanding — eat where the locals eat

---

## SkyNest AI Copilot Tips
Ask: "Design a 7-day Thailand trip combining Bangkok temples, Chiang Mai culture, and Koh Samui beach" — SkyNest AI can arrange domestic Thai flights, hotels, and transfers in one seamless bundle.

# Dubai — SkyNest Destination Guide

## Overview
Dubai is the city of superlatives — the world's tallest building, the largest shopping mall, man-made islands shaped like a palm tree. Once a fishing village, it is now a global hub for business, luxury, and increasingly, culture and adventure. SkyNest's most popular destination.

## Hub Airport
**Dubai International (DXB)** — Terminal 2 for SkyNest arrivals. One of the world's busiest airports.

---

## Best Time to Visit
| Season | Months | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Peak / Ideal | Nov – Mar | 20–28°C; outdoor activities comfortable; Dubai Shopping Festival (Dec–Jan) |
| Shoulder | Apr, Oct | Warm but manageable |
| Off-peak / Hot | May – Sep | 35–45°C; mostly indoor activities; lowest hotel prices |

---

## Must-See Attractions

Dubai — SkyNest Destination Guide

**Icons**: Burj Khalifa (observation decks At The Top and At The Top SKY), Palm Jumeirah, Burj Al Arab exterior
**Shopping**: Dubai Mall (world's largest), Mall of the Emirates (indoor ski slope), Gold Souk, Spice Souk in Deira
**Culture**: Al Fahidi Historic District (old Dubai), Dubai Frame, Museum of the Future
**Outdoors**: Desert safari (4x4 dune drive, camel ride, BBQ dinner under stars), Hatta mountain pools, Kite Beach
**Theme Parks**: Dubai Parks & Resorts (Legoland, Motiongate), IMG Worlds of Adventure

---

## Neighbourhoods
- **Downtown Dubai**: Burj Khalifa, Dubai Fountain, high-end dining
- **Dubai Marina**: Waterfront living, JBR beach walk, nightlife
- **Deira / Bur Dubai**: Old city, souks, affordable stays
- **Palm Jumeirah**: Luxury resorts, private beaches, Atlantis
- **Business Bay**: Corporate hub, boutique restaurants

---

Dubai — SkyNest Destination Guide

## Getting Around
- **Dubai Metro**: Red and Green lines cover major tourist areas; ~AED 3–8 per trip
- **Taxis**: Metered, affordable (~AED 10 flagfall); Careem app available
- **SkyNest Car Rental**: Available at DXB terminal; see Dubai Cars section

---

## Currency
UAE Dirham (AED). USD 1 ≈ AED 3.67 (pegged). ATMs everywhere. Cards widely accepted. Cash useful for souks.

---

## Practical Tips
- Dress modestly in souks and religious sites; swimwear only on beaches and pools
- Ramadan: restaurants may be closed daytime; alcohol restricted during Ramadan month
- Friday/Saturday is the UAE weekend — some government services closed
- Tipping: 10–15% at restaurants is appreciated (not mandatory)

---

## SkyNest AI Copilot Tips
Ask: "Plan a 5-day Dubai trip with desert safari, beach day, and luxury dining" — SkyNest AI will bundle your hotel, transfers, and activities.

# India — SkyNest Destination Guide

## Overview
India is a subcontinent of extraordinary diversity — ancient temples beside glass towers, spice markets filling narrow alleys, and landscapes ranging from Himalayan peaks to tropical beaches. SkyNest operates out of four Indian gateway cities: Mumbai, Delhi, Bangalore, and Chennai.

## Top Cities & Regions Accessible via SkyNest Hubs

### Mumbai (Bombay)
The financial and entertainment capital. Home to Bollywood, Marine Drive, the Gateway of India, and world-class restaurants. Base airport: **Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj International (BOM)**.

### Delhi (New Delhi)
India's capital — a living museum spanning Mughal splendour (Red Fort, Qutub Minar, Humayun's Tomb) and modern India (Connaught Place, Cyber City Gurgaon). Base airport: **Indira Gandhi International (DEL)**.

### Bangalore (Bengaluru)
India's Silicon Valley — cosmopolitan, green, and tech-forward with a thriving café culture, craft beer scene, and proximity to Mysore and Coorg. Base airport: **Kempegowda International (BLR)**.

metadata.title: India — SkyNest Destination Guide
headings: Top Cities & Regions Accessible via SkyNest Hubs

page_content:
India — SkyNest Destination Guide

### Chennai
Gateway to Tamil Nadu — classical Carnatic music, Dravidian temple architecture, pristine Marina Beach, and the cultural depth of South India. Base airport: **Chennai International (MAA)**.

---

## Best Time to Visit
| Season | Months | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Winter (Best) | Oct – Feb | Cool, dry across most regions; peak tourist season |
| Spring | Mar – Apr | Pleasant in the hills; hot in plains |
| Monsoon | Jun – Sep | Lush and green; expect heavy rain across the west coast |
| Summer | Apr – Jun | Hot across most of India; hill stations popular |

---

## Must-See Attractions

**North India**: Taj Mahal (Agra), Varanasi ghats, Jaisalmer desert fort, Rishikesh yoga retreat
**South India**: Meenakshi Temple (Madurai), Hampi ruins, Kerala backwaters, Mysore Palace
**West India**: Mumbai's Elephanta Caves, Goa beaches, Rann of Kutch salt flats
**East India**: Darjeeling tea estates, Sundarbans mangroves, Puri Jagannath Temple

---

India — SkyNest Destination Guide

## Getting Around
- **Domestic flights**: IndiGo, Air India, SpiceJet connect all major cities
- **Trains**: Indian Railways — book at irctc.co.in; iconic routes include Delhi–Jaipur, Mumbai–Goa
- **Ride-hailing**: Ola and Uber available in all major cities
- **Auto-rickshaws**: For short distances; negotiate or use meter

---

## Currency
Indian Rupee (INR). USD 1 ≈ INR 83 (2026). ATMs widely available. UPI payments (Google Pay, PhonePe) nearly universal.

---

## Useful Phrases (Hindi)
- Hello: Namaste
- Thank you: Dhanyavaad
- How much?: Kitna hai?
- Delicious!: Bahut tasty hai!

---

## SkyNest AI Copilot Tips
Ask SkyNest AI Copilot: "Build me a 10-day India itinerary starting in Delhi, ending in Goa" for a personalised day-by-day plan with hotel and transport recommendations.

# London — SkyNest Destination Guide

## Overview
London is one of the world's great capitals — a city of 2,000 years of history where ancient Roman walls stand beside glass skyscrapers, where free world-class museums line the Thames, and where every neighbourhood feels like a different city. SkyNest connects India, Dubai, Singapore, Paris, New York, and Bangkok directly to London Heathrow.

## Hub Airport
**London Heathrow (LHR)** — Terminal 3 for SkyNest arrivals. Heathrow Express to Paddington: 15 minutes, £25. Tube: ~45–60 min, £6 (Oyster card).

---

## Best Time to Visit
| Season | Months | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Summer (Peak) | Jun – Aug | Warm (18–25°C), long days; festivals, outdoor events; most expensive |
| Spring | Mar – May | Lovely (cherry blossoms in parks), increasingly busy |
| Autumn | Sep – Nov | Cool, beautiful foliage; fewer crowds |
| Winter | Dec – Feb | Cold but festive (Christmas lights, markets); cheapest hotels |

---

## Must-See Attractions

London — SkyNest Destination Guide

**History**: Tower of London, Buckingham Palace, Westminster Abbey, St Paul's Cathedral, Greenwich Royal Observatory
**World-Class Free Museums**: British Museum, Natural History Museum, V&A Museum, Tate Modern, National Gallery, Science Museum
**Neighbourhoods**: Shoreditch (street art, tech), Notting Hill (colourful townhouses, Portobello Market), Soho (dining, nightlife), South Bank (Thames walk, food market)
**Entertainment**: West End theatre (Book of Mormon, Les Misérables, Hamilton), Ronnie Scott's jazz club, Camden Market
**Day Trips**: Bath (Roman baths, Georgian architecture), Oxford/Cambridge, Cotswolds villages, Windsor Castle

---

London — SkyNest Destination Guide

## Getting Around
- **London Underground (Tube)**: 11 lines, covers almost everything; buy Oyster card or use contactless
- **Buses**: Double-decker iconic; contactless payment
- **Elizabeth Line**: Fast route across the city (Heathrow to Paddington in 15 min)
- **Black cabs**: Metered; hail on street or use the Gett app
- **Uber**: Available and reliable
- **SkyNest Car Rental**: Available at LHR Terminal 3; note: driving on the left; Congestion Charge in central London (£15/day)

---

## Currency
British Pound (GBP). USD 1 ≈ GBP 0.79 (2026). Cards universally accepted; many places are now cashless.

---

## Practical Tips
- UK uses 240V electricity; bring UK plug adapters (Type G, 3-pin)
- Stand on the right on escalators; walk on the left
- Service charge (12.5%) often added to restaurant bills; check before tipping additionally
- Pub culture: ordering at the bar is the norm; no table service in traditional pubs

---

London — SkyNest Destination Guide

## SkyNest AI Copilot Tips
Ask: "Plan a 7-day London trip including a Cotswolds day trip and West End show" — SkyNest AI will suggest hotels near your top attractions and pre-book theatre options.

# New York City — SkyNest Destination Guide

## Overview
New York City is the city that never sleeps — five boroughs, 8 million people, 800 languages, and more world-class museums, restaurants, and live performances than most countries combined. Whether you want Broadway, the best pizza on Earth, fine dining at Per Se, or sunrise from the Brooklyn Bridge, NYC delivers.

## Hub Airport
**John F. Kennedy International (JFK)** — Terminal 7 for SkyNest arrivals. AirTrain to Jamaica LIRR/Subway: $8.25 + subway/LIRR fare. Taxi to Midtown Manhattan: flat rate $70 + tolls + tip (~$85 total). Uber/Lyft: $45–70 typical.

---

## Best Time to Visit
| Season | Months | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Spring | Apr – Jun | Beautiful, comfortable; Central Park blooms |
| Autumn (Best) | Sep – Nov | Ideal weather; colours in Central Park; less humid |
| Summer | Jun – Aug | Hot, humid; open-air events, rooftop bars peak season |
| Winter | Dec – Feb | Cold; iconic Christmas (Rockefeller tree, skating); New Year's Eve Times Square |

---

## Must-See Attractions

metadata.title: New York City — SkyNest Destination Guide
headings: New York City — SkyNest Destination Guide

page_content:
New York City — SkyNest Destination Guide

**Iconic NYC**: Statue of Liberty + Ellis Island (book ferry weeks ahead), Central Park (843 acres), Empire State Building, Brooklyn Bridge (walk across), Times Square, High Line elevated park
**World-Class Museums**: Metropolitan Museum of Art (Met — free suggested donation), MoMA, American Museum of Natural History, Whitney Museum, Guggenheim
**Neighbourhoods**: Manhattan (Midtown, SoHo, Tribeca, Harlem, Upper West Side), Brooklyn (DUMBO, Williamsburg, Park Slope), Astoria (Queens — best food diversity)
**Food**: Bagels and lox (Russ & Daughters), New York slice pizza (Joe's Pizza), pastrami sandwich (Katz's Deli), oysters at Grand Central Oyster Bar, omakase in the East Village
**Entertainment**: Broadway shows (book 6+ months for top shows), jazz at the Village Vanguard, comedy at Carolines, rooftop bars with skyline views

---

metadata.title: New York City — SkyNest Destination Guide

page_content:
New York City — SkyNest Destination Guide

## Getting Around
- **Subway**: 24/7 operation; $2.90 per ride; buy OMNY card or use contactless credit card
- **Buses**: Good for crosstown trips; slow in traffic
- **Walking**: Most iconic way to see Manhattan; numbered streets make navigation easy
- **Citi Bike (bike share)**: Great for Hudson River Greenway and flat areas
- **Taxis / Uber**: Reliable; surge pricing during peak hours
- **SkyNest Car Rental**: Available at JFK; driving in Manhattan strongly discouraged (traffic, parking)

---

## Currency
US Dollar (USD). Cards universally accepted; tipping (18–20% at restaurants, 15% for taxis) is culturally standard and not optional in social terms.

---

## Practical Tips
- NYC is expensive — budget $150–300/day per person for mid-range experience
- Power outlets: 120V, Type A/B (two flat pins); bring adapters for UK/European/Indian plugs
- Safety has dramatically improved; standard city awareness is sufficient
- Tourist Information Centers at Midtown (810 7th Ave) and City Hall area

---

# Paris — SkyNest Destination Guide

## Overview
Paris is the city of light, love, and art — where the Eiffel Tower glimmers at night, where every arrondissement has a neighbourhood bistro serving perfect steak frites, and where world-class museums could occupy a lifetime of visits. SkyNest connects Delhi, Dubai, Singapore, London, New York, and Bangkok directly to Charles de Gaulle.

## Hub Airport
**Paris Charles de Gaulle (CDG)** — Terminal 2E for SkyNest arrivals. RER B train to city centre: 35 minutes, €11.80. Roissybus to Opéra: 60 minutes, €16.10. Taxi to central Paris: ~€55 (fixed flat rate).

---

## Best Time to Visit
| Season | Months | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Spring (Best) | Apr – Jun | Mild (15–22°C), blooming parks, long days |
| Summer | Jul – Aug | Warm but busy; August locals leave, tourists arrive |
| Autumn | Sep – Nov | Golden light, shorter queues, harvest season |
| Winter | Dec – Jan | Cold but festive; Christmas markets, fewer tourists |

**Fashion Week**: Jan/Feb (Haute Couture), Mar (Womenswear), Jun (Menswear), Oct (Womenswear) — hotels peak during these weeks.

---

Paris — SkyNest Destination Guide

## Must-See Attractions

**Icons**: Eiffel Tower (book timed entry 2+ months ahead), Louvre Museum (Mona Lisa, Venus de Milo), Notre-Dame Cathedral (reopened 2025 after restoration), Arc de Triomphe, Montmartre / Sacré-Cœur
**Art**: Musée d'Orsay (Impressionists), Centre Pompidou (modern art), Musée Rodin, Palais Royal
**Neighbourhoods**: Le Marais (Jewish quarter, boutiques), Saint-Germain-des-Prés (literary cafés), Canal Saint-Martin (hipster), Montmartre (artists, vineyards)
**Food & Drink**: Croissant at any boulangerie (the real test of a neighbourhood), escargot and steak tartare, macarons from Pierre Hermé, fine dining on the Seine (Lapérouse, Le Grand Véfour)
**Day Trips**: Palace of Versailles (30 min by RER), Champagne region (Reims, 1.5 hours TGV), Loire Valley châteaux (2 hours TGV)

---

Paris — SkyNest Destination Guide

## Getting Around
- **Métro**: 16 lines, very efficient; Navigo Liberté+ card recommended for multi-day visits
- **RER Trains**: Suburban rail; connects CDG, Versailles, Disneyland Paris
- **Vélib'**: Bike-share scheme, 1,400 stations city-wide; practical and scenic
- **Walking**: Paris's most romantic and practical option for central sightseeing
- **SkyNest Car Rental**: Available at CDG; note Paris centre driving not recommended (Crit'Air sticker required for most zones)

---

## Currency
Euro (€). USD 1 ≈ €0.92 (2026). Cards widely accepted; some boulangeries and markets prefer cash.

---

## Practical Tips
- Many attractions require timed-entry booking — never just show up at the Louvre
- Learn a few French phrases; Parisians deeply appreciate the effort
- Lunch menus (formule) at restaurants offer great value: starter + main + dessert for €15–25
- Pickpocketing common around Eiffel Tower and major tourist sites — use front pockets or crossbody bags

---

Paris — SkyNest Destination Guide

## SkyNest AI Copilot Tips
Ask: "What's a romantic 5-day Paris itinerary avoiding tourist traps?" or "Find me a boutique hotel near the Marais with Eiffel Tower views under $200/night."

# Singapore — SkyNest Destination Guide

## Overview
Singapore is a city-state where gleaming skyscrapers share the skyline with colonial shophouses, where hawker centres serve world-class food for $4, and where the Gardens by the Bay glow neon every night. Safe, clean, efficient, and endlessly surprising.

## Hub Airport
**Singapore Changi Airport (SIN)** — Consistently ranked world's best airport. Jewel Changi with its indoor waterfall is worth visiting even without a flight.

---

## Best Time to Visit
Singapore has a tropical climate with no distinct dry season. Rain can come any time.
| Period | Notes |
|---|---|
| Feb – Apr | Relatively drier; comfortable |
| Jun – Aug | Occasional haze from regional fires; Formula 1 GP in Sep |
| Dec – Jan | Slightly wetter but festive — Christmas lights on Orchard Road |

---

## Must-See Attractions

Singapore — SkyNest Destination Guide

**Icons**: Marina Bay Sands (infinity pool), Gardens by the Bay (Supertree Grove, Cloud Forest, Flower Dome), Merlion Park
**Culture & Heritage**: Chinatown (Buddha Tooth Relic Temple), Little India (Mustafa Centre), Arab Street (Sultan Mosque), Colonial District (Raffles Hotel, National Museum)
**Nature**: Singapore Botanic Gardens (UNESCO), MacRitchie Reservoir treetop walk, Sentosa Island beaches
**Food**: Maxwell Food Centre, Lau Pa Sat, Newton Food Centre, Hawker Chan (Michelin-starred hawker)
**Shopping**: Orchard Road, Bugis Street, VivoCity, Haji Lane boutiques

---

## Neighbourhoods
- **Marina Bay**: CBD, luxury, iconic skyline
- **Chinatown / Tanjong Pagar**: Heritage and hipster food
- **Orchard Road**: Shopping hub
- **Kampong Glam**: Malay culture, vintage shops
- **East Coast**: Local residential, beaches, seafood

---

Singapore — SkyNest Destination Guide

## Getting Around
- **MRT (Mass Rapid Transit)**: Extensive, efficient, ~SGD 0.90–2.20 per trip; EZ-Link card recommended
- **Buses**: Comprehensive network; same card as MRT
- **Grab (ride-hailing)**: App-based, reliable, ~SGD 8–20 for most city journeys
- **Walking**: Many areas very walkable; covered walkways between many buildings

---

## Currency
Singapore Dollar (SGD). USD 1 ≈ SGD 1.34 (2026). Cards widely accepted; hawker centres prefer cash.

---

## Practical Tips
- Fines for chewing gum, littering, and jaywalking are real — take them seriously
- Tipping not customary or expected
- Street food is safe and excellent — do not skip hawker centres
- GST (9%) applies to most goods and services; Tourist Refund Scheme available for purchases over SGD 100

---

## SkyNest AI Copilot Tips
Ask: "What are the best hawker stalls near Marina Bay?" or "Design a family 4-day Singapore itinerary with Universal Studios and Sentosa."

# Tokyo — SkyNest Destination Guide

## Overview
Tokyo is a city of extraordinary contradictions — ancient shrines in the shadow of neon towers, Michelin stars in basement ramen shops, silence in Buddhist temples a block from the world's busiest pedestrian crossing. Japan's capital is one of the safest, most efficient, and most endlessly fascinating cities on Earth.

## Hub Airport
**Tokyo Narita International (NRT)** — Terminal 1 for SkyNest arrivals. Narita Express (N'EX) to Shinjuku/Tokyo station: 60 minutes, ¥3,070. Limousine Bus to major hotels: ¥3,600.

---

## Best Time to Visit
| Season | Months | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Spring (Cherry Blossom) | Late Mar – Apr | Most popular; extraordinary beauty; book 4+ months ahead |
| Autumn (Foliage) | Nov | Red and gold foliage; equally stunning; less crowded than spring |
| Summer | Jun – Aug | Hot and humid; festivals (Obon); some great fireworks |
| Winter | Dec – Feb | Cold but festive; illuminations; Christmas–New Year |

---

## Must-See Attractions

Tokyo — SkyNest Destination Guide

**Iconic Tokyo**: Shibuya Crossing (world's busiest), Senso-ji Temple (Asakusa), Meiji Shrine, Tokyo Skytree (634m view), teamLab Planets (digital art)
**Neighbourhoods**: Shinjuku (neon, Golden Gai), Harajuku (fashion, Takeshita Street), Akihabara (electronics, anime), Shimokitazawa (vintage, indie music), Yanaka (old Tokyo, temple cats)
**Food**: Tsukiji outer market (breakfast sushi), Ichiran Ramen (24/7 solo booth ramen), Depachika (department store basement food halls), Omakase sushi (reserve months in advance)
**Day Trips**: Nikko (ornate shrines, mountain scenery, 2 hours), Hakone (Mt Fuji views, hot springs, 90 minutes), Kamakura (Great Buddha, temples, 1 hour), Kyoto (cultural heartland, 2.5 hours by Shinkansen)

---

Tokyo — SkyNest Destination Guide

## Getting Around
- **IC Card (Suica / Pasmo)**: Load credit and tap on any train, subway, bus; also works at convenience stores and vending machines
- **Tokyo Metro + Toei Subway**: Near-perfect coverage of central Tokyo; very frequent
- **JR Yamanote Line**: Loop line connecting major areas (Shinjuku, Shibuya, Harajuku, Akihabara, Tokyo, Ueno)
- **Shinkansen (Bullet Train)**: From Tokyo station to Kyoto (2h 15m, ¥14,170), Osaka, Hiroshima
- **Taxis**: Metered, honest, expensive (~¥700 flagfall)
- **SkyNest Car Rental**: Available at NRT; note: driving on the left; International Driving Permit required

---

## Currency
Japanese Yen (JPY). USD 1 ≈ JPY 150 (2026). Cash still widely used — many smaller establishments card-free. Convenience store ATMs (7-Eleven, Japan Post) accept foreign cards.

---

Tokyo — SkyNest Destination Guide

## Practical Tips
- Quiet on trains — no phone calls; eating discouraged on most lines
- Bow slightly when thanked; remove shoes before entering homes and some traditional restaurants (tatami rooms)
- Rubbish bins are rare — carry your waste until you find one (konbini have bins)
- Get a pocket Wi-Fi or SIM at Narita airport (essential for navigation)
- 7-Eleven, FamilyMart, Lawson: arguably the world's best convenience stores

---

## SkyNest AI Copilot Tips
Ask: "Design a 10-day Japan itinerary including Tokyo, Kyoto, and Hakone for a first-time visitor" — SkyNest AI includes bullet train bookings, hotel recommendations, and a daily plan.

# SkyNest Airlines — Baggage Policy

**Effective Date**: January 1, 2026

---

## 1. Cabin Baggage (Hand Luggage)

### Economy Class
- **Quantity**: 1 cabin bag + 1 personal item (handbag, laptop bag, or small backpack)
- **Cabin Bag Size**: Max 55 × 40 × 20 cm (including handles and wheels)
- **Cabin Bag Weight**: Max 7 kg
- **Personal Item Size**: Max 35 × 25 × 15 cm
- **Personal Item Weight**: Max 3 kg

### Business Class
- **Quantity**: 2 cabin bags + 1 personal item
- **Each Cabin Bag**: Max 55 × 40 × 20 cm, max 10 kg per bag

### First Class
- **Quantity**: 2 cabin bags + 1 personal item
- **Each Cabin Bag**: Max 55 × 40 × 20 cm, max 12 kg per bag

---

## 2. Checked Baggage Allowance

### Economy — Saver Fare
| Route Type | Free Allowance | Fee for Extra Bag |
|---|---|---|
| Short-haul (< 4 hours) | 0 kg (no free checked bag) | $35 for first bag (up to 23 kg) |
| Medium-haul (4–8 hours) | 23 kg (1 bag) | $45 for second bag (up to 23 kg) |
| Long-haul (8+ hours) | 23 kg (1 bag) | $60 for second bag (up to 23 kg) |

SkyNest Airlines — Baggage Policy

### Economy — Flexi Fare
| Route Type | Free Allowance | Fee for Extra Bag |
|---|---|---|
| Short-haul | 23 kg (1 bag) | $35 for second bag |
| Medium-haul | 30 kg (1 or 2 bags, combined) | $45 for third bag |
| Long-haul | 46 kg (2 bags, up to 23 kg each) | $60 for third bag |

### Business Class
| Route Type | Free Allowance |
|---|---|
| All routes | 2 bags × 32 kg each (64 kg total) |

### First Class
| Route Type | Free Allowance |
|---|---|
| All routes | 3 bags × 32 kg each (96 kg total) |

---

## 3. Excess Baggage Fees

Bags exceeding the free allowance will be charged:
- **Economy (Saver)**: $9 per kg over limit
- **Economy (Flexi)**: $8 per kg over limit
- **Business**: $6 per kg over limit

Maximum weight per individual bag: **32 kg**. Bags over 32 kg must be split into two.

---

## 4. Sports and Special Equipment

SkyNest Airlines — Baggage Policy

| Item | Policy |
|---|---|
| Bicycle | Accepted; must be boxed; $60 fee per direction |
| Golf clubs | Accepted; bag + clubs max 23 kg; $50 fee per direction |
| Surfboard / kiteboard | Accepted with advance notice; $70 fee per direction |
| Skis / snowboard | Accepted; $55 fee per direction |
| Musical instruments (large) | Instrument may need to purchase an additional seat if over 7 kg; contact care@skynest.aero |
| Prams / strollers | Free of charge; gate-checked |
| Wheelchairs and mobility aids | Always free; contact accessibility@skynest.aero 48 hours before flight |

---

## 5. Prohibited Items

The following are **never** permitted in cabin or checked baggage:
- Explosives, fireworks, flares of any kind
- Flammable liquids (gasoline, lighter fluid) beyond travel allowance
- Guns, firearms, or weapons (unless specifically pre-authorised for checked baggage in compliance with local law)
- Lithium batteries over 160 Wh (some over 100 Wh also restricted in checked baggage — check regulations)

Carry-on liquids: 100 ml containers only, in a resealable transparent bag (1 litre capacity).

SkyNest Airlines — Baggage Policy

---

## 6. Lost, Damaged, or Delayed Baggage

- Report at the airport **before leaving the arrivals hall** — claims cannot be accepted after.
- Compensation limit (Montreal Convention): **$1,600 per passenger**.
- Delayed baggage: SkyNest will deliver to your hotel or address at no cost within 24 hours of arrival of your delayed bag.
- For higher-value items, purchase travel insurance with baggage cover before flying.

---

## 7. SkyNest Miles Member Benefits

| Tier | Extra Allowance |
|---|---|
| Silver | +7 kg on Economy medium/long-haul |
| Gold | +10 kg on all routes |
| Platinum | 1 additional free checked bag on all routes |
| Elite | 2 additional free checked bags, no excess fees |

# SkyNest Airlines — Cancellation & Change Policy

**Effective Date**: January 1, 2026

---

## 1. Flight Changes (Date / Time / Route)

### Economy — Saver Fare
- **Change fee**: $75 per person per sector
- **Fare difference**: Any fare difference at time of rebooking must be paid
- **Changes permitted until**: Up to 4 hours before scheduled departure
- **No same-day changes** permitted on Saver fare

### Economy — Flexi Fare
- **Change fee**: $0 (changes are free)
- **Fare difference**: Must be paid if new fare is higher; difference refunded if new fare is lower
- **Changes permitted until**: Up to 1 hour before scheduled departure

### Business Class
- **Change fee**: $0 on all changes
- **Fare difference**: Applies
- **Same-day changes**: Permitted subject to seat availability

### First Class
- **Change fee**: $0 on all changes; full flexibility with no conditions

---

## 2. Flight Cancellations (Passenger-Initiated)

SkyNest Airlines — Cancellation & Change Policy

| Fare / Timing | Refund Amount |
|---|---|
| Economy Saver — 72+ hours before | 50% of base fare (taxes returned in full) |
| Economy Saver — within 72 hours | No refund (travel credit 15% of fare, valid 12 months) |
| Economy Flexi — any time before departure | 100% refund minus $45 processing fee |
| Economy Flexi — within 24 hours of booking | 100% refund, no fee (if departure 7+ days away) |
| Business Class — any time before departure | 100% refund minus $75 processing fee |
| Business Class — within 24 hours of booking | 100% refund, no fee |
| First Class — any time before departure | 100% refund minus $100 processing fee |
| No-show (any fare) | No refund; travel credit per fare rules below |

### No-Show Travel Credits
- Economy Saver no-show: 20% credit of fare paid (valid 12 months)
- Economy Flexi no-show: 30% credit of fare paid (valid 12 months)
- Business no-show: 30% of base fare as SkyNest Miles
- First no-show: 40% of base fare as SkyNest Miles

---

## 3. Airline-Initiated Cancellations and Disruptions

SkyNest Airlines — Cancellation & Change Policy

If **SkyNest** cancels, delays 3+ hours, or significantly reschedules:

| Disruption Type | Passenger Entitlement |
|---|---|
| Cancellation | Full refund (all charges) OR free rebook + $300 voucher |
| Delay 3–6 hours | $50 voucher |
| Delay 6+ hours | $150 voucher + lounge access |
| Delay 12+ hours | $300 voucher + hotel accommodation (if overnight delay) |

---

## 4. Special Circumstances (Force Majeure)

In cases of extraordinary circumstances outside SkyNest's control — natural disasters, war, pandemic travel bans, severe weather — SkyNest will offer:
- Free rebooking within 12 months (same origin/destination)
- Travel credit for the full fare value (no expiry within force majeure period)

---

## 5. How to Cancel or Change a Booking

**Online (fastest)**:
1. Go to **My Bookings** at www.skynest.aero or in the SkyNest app
2. Select your booking → **Change** or **Cancel**
3. Follow prompts; refund/credit confirmed within 1 hour

**Customer Care**: 1800-SKY-NEST | care@skynest.aero  
**At airport**: Visit SkyNest check-in counter before check-in closes

---

## 6. Loyalty Member Benefits

| CHML | Child Meal (for passengers under 10) |
| BBML | Baby Meal (for infants; jars/purees) |

---

### Beverage Policy

**Economy**: Complimentary soft drinks, juices, water, tea/coffee. Alcoholic beverages available for purchase ($8–$12 per drink) on routes 3+ hours.

**Business Class**: Full complimentary bar including wines, champagne, spirits, cocktails, and premium non-alcoholic beverages.

page_content:
SkyNest Airlines — Meal Policy & Loyalty Benefits

**First Class**: Personal cocktail bar, curated wine list (updated quarterly with SkyNest sommelier), Champagne on demand.

---

### SkyNest Signature Dishes (Business Class)

SkyNest partners with renowned chefs to create rotating in-flight menus inspired by destination cultures:

- **Mumbai–London**: Butter Chicken Risotto (SkyNest Fusion) / Beef Wellington Bites with truffle jus
- **Dubai–Paris**: Lamb Kofta with Persian saffron rice / Crème Brûlée with cardamom
- **Singapore–Tokyo**: Laksa-inspired ramen with wagyu / Yuzu tart
- **London–New York**: Classic British roast / New York cheesecake in-flight edition
- **Mumbai–Tokyo**: Prawn masala bao / Matcha opera cake

Menus rotate every 8 weeks. Full current menu available at www.skynest.aero/inflight-dining.

---

## Part B: SkyNest Miles Loyalty Benefits on Food & Dining

### Earn Miles at Restaurants

SkyNest has partnered with 200+ airport restaurants globally. Dine at a partner restaurant and earn:
- **Blue / Silver**: 2 miles per $1 spent
- **Gold**: 4 miles per $1 spent
- **Platinum / Elite**: 6 miles per $1 spent

headings: Part B: SkyNest Miles Loyalty Benefits on Food & Dining

page_content:
SkyNest Airlines — Meal Policy & Loyalty Benefits

Scan your SkyNest Miles card or app QR code at payment.

### Partner Airport Lounges

| Tier | Lounge Access |
|---|---|
| Blue / Silver | Not included (pay per visit: $35 / use 3,500 miles) |
| Gold | Access on long-haul routes (1 guest included) |
| Platinum | Access on all routes (1 guest included) |
| Elite | Unlimited access on all routes (up to 2 guests) |

**Lounges include**: Hot and cold buffet, live cooking stations, espresso bars, cocktail bar, shower facilities, business centre, sleeping pods.

### Pre-Order In-Flight Meal Upgrade

Any passenger may pre-order a premium meal upgrade 48+ hours before departure:
- Economy → Business-grade meal: $28 per meal
- Business → First Class-grade meal (where First Class operates): $55 per meal
- Payment via SkyNest wallet, card, or Miles (2,800 / 5,500 miles respectively)

### Dietary Preference Memory

SkyNest's AI Copilot remembers your meal preferences and automatically applies them to future bookings (opt-in via Settings → Meal Preferences in the app). Gold members and above have this activated by default.

- Biometrics required at VFS Global centre
- 90-day rule applies across ALL Schengen countries combined, not just France
- Required documents: return flights, hotel booking, bank statements, travel insurance (€30,000)
